title: At-least-once vs exactly-once delivery
description: Most queues guarantee at-least-once delivery; exactly-once is achieved by idempotent handlers, not by the transport.
body: Use when reasoning about duplicate processing. Practical messaging (SQS, most Kafka setups, RabbitMQ) is at-least-once: a message can be redelivered after a consumer crash between effect and ack. Do not assume the broker gives true exactly-once end to end. Actions: achieve effective exactly-once by making the handler idempotent on the message id (dedup store or idempotent upsert) so redelivery is harmless. Validation signals include a redelivery test asserting one effect. Risk boundary: relying on broker 'exactly-once' marketing for a money path without handler-level dedup is unsafe and should be escalated.
